input.background: Consider a standby system with two identical valves each with constant failure rate $\lambda=10^{-5}$ failures/hours. The probability $\mathrm{p}_{\mathrm{s}}$ that the switch $S$ will activate the standby valve is 0.995.
input.question: What is the system MTTF (Mean Time To Failure) for the non-identical components scenario?

output.answer: The system MTTF is given by $$\begin{aligned} M T T F & =\int_0^{\infty} R(t) d t=\int_0^{\infty}\left(e^{-\lambda t}+\frac{p_s \lambda_1}{\lambda_1-\lambda_2} e^{-\lambda_2 t}-\frac{p_s \lambda_1}{\lambda_1-\lambda_2} e^{-\lambda_1 t}\right) d t \\ & =\frac{1}{\lambda_1}+\frac{p_s}{\lambda_2} \end{aligned}$$
